encounter.reason: Six-week postpartum routine assessment following April 2022 vaginal delivery
encounter.location: Lone Star Women's Health, Austin, TX

labs.name: fasting glucose
labs.notes: Confirms normalization of glucose metabolism postpartum

labs.name: hemoglobin A1c
labs.notes: Consistent with resolution of gestational diabetes after delivery

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening
screening.notes: Normal, negative for depression. Fatigue appears related to newborn care demands rather than mood disorder.

conditions.name: gestational diabetes mellitus
conditions.notes: Status changed from active to resolved postpartum. Managed during pregnancy with dietary modification. Confirmation based on normal fasting glucose (88 mg/dL) and normal A1c (5.4%) at 6 weeks postpartum, indicating complete metabolic normalization.

document_references.description: Comprehensive 6-week postpartum assessment documenting normal recovery and resolution of gestational diabetes. Note documents vaginal delivery of healthy female infant (Sofia) in April 2022, current vital signs (BP 110/74, HR 70, temp 98.0°F, RR 14, SpO2 98%), and review of postpartum symptoms (breastfeeding well-established, lochia resolved, minimal perineal discomfort). Postpartum metabolic follow-up confirms resolution of gestational diabetes with normal fasting glucose and A1c. PHQ-2 screening reassuring for mood. Exam confirms normal postpartum recovery without hemorrhage, infection, or other complications. Assessment and plan address annual diabetes screening recommendation due to elevated lifetime type 2 risk from gestational diabetes history, counseling on contraception and future reproductive planning, and safety-net instructions for return precautions.
